# Quoting evasion: the parser resolves single-quoted 'rm' to command name rm.
'rm' -rf /	red
# Wrapper stripping: command, env, nice all get stripped by the walker.
command rm -rf /	red
env rm -rf /	red
nice rm -rf /	red
# Chained wrappers: sudo is stripped, then env is stripped, then rm -rf / matches.
sudo env rm -rf /	red
# Double-quoted command names: parser resolves quotes.
"rm" -rf /	red
# Semicolon chaining: rm -rf is a separate command and triggers RED.
echo safe; rm -rf /	red
# Command substitution in command position: unresolvable → yellow.
$(echo rm) -rf /	yellow
# Backslash evasion: \rm is treated as literal command name by mvdan.cc/sh.
# Known M2 gap — does not match rm RED rules. Falls to default yellow.
\rm -rf /	yellow
